description:
You are a QA engineer who has just finished preparing a small set of test-environment configuration files located in 
 /home/user/test_configs/  

 Your manager asks you to:
 1. Create a compressed “tar.gz” archive that contains every file that is currently inside /home/user/test_configs/  
 2. Place that archive in a new directory called /home/user/backups/  
 3. Write a one-line log entry confirming success to a **brand-new** file named /home/user/backup_log.txt  

 The required archive and log entry must satisfy all of the following constraints so that an automated checker can validate your work:

 Archive requirements  
 • Full path: /home/user/backups/test_configs_backup.tar.gz  
 • The archive must be a “tar.gz” (i.e., `tar` with gzip compression).  
 • The archive must contain exactly the same directory layout that exists under /home/user/test_configs/ (no additional leading directories, no omitted files).  
 • The files must appear in the archive with these exact relative paths (nothing else):  
   – config1.ini  
   – config2.ini  

 Log-file requirements  
 • Full path: /home/user/backup_log.txt  
 • The file must consist of **one** line, terminated with a single newline (`\n`).  
 • Content format (exactly one space after the colon):  
   ARCHIVE_CREATED: /home/user/backups/test_configs_backup.tar.gz  

 Example (including the trailing newline visualised as ⏎):  
   ARCHIVE_CREATED: /home/user/backups/test_configs_backup.tar.gz⏎  

 Additional notes  
 • Only standard GNU/Linux user-level tools are needed (tar, gzip, mkdir, etc.).  
 • You do not need to show or suppress command output; default behaviour is fine.  
 • Create any directories that do not yet exist.  
 • Overwrite any existing /home/user/backup_log.txt or archive of the same name if they already exist.  
 • The entire task can be completed with about two or three shell commands.
truth:
Initial setup before the agent starts:  
 1. Directory /home/user/test_configs/ exists and contains two files:  
    • /home/user/test_configs/config1.ini – contents:  
        [database]  
        host=localhost  
        port=5432  
    • /home/user/test_configs/config2.ini – contents:  
        [web]  
        host=0.0.0.0  
        port=8080  
 2. Directory /home/user/backups/ does **not** exist.  
 3. File /home/user/backup_log.txt does **not** exist.  

 Verification criteria after the agent finishes:  
 1. Directory /home/user/backups/ exists with normal user read/write permissions.  
 2. File /home/user/backups/test_configs_backup.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
    • Running `tar -tzf /home/user/backups/test_configs_backup.tar.gz` outputs exactly:  
      config1.ini  
      config2.ini  
      (each followed by a newline; no leading “./” or parent directory paths).  
 3. /home/user/backup_log.txt exists, is world-readable, and contains exactly one line:  
      ARCHIVE_CREATED: /home/user/backups/test_configs_backup.tar.gz  
    followed by a single newline as its only trailing character.  
 4. No extra files are present in /home/user/backups/.
name: task_000000_b9344aaa
